text.title: Docker Core Operations Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Dockerfile MUST use multi-stage builds: build tools live in a builder stage and never in the runtime stage.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Runtime stage MUST set `USER` to a non-root account; running as UID 0 is rejected.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Image MUST declare a `HEALTHCHECK` instruction (or document why none is possible).
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Layer order MUST place dependency installation BEFORE source COPY so dependency layers cache across code changes.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Build secrets MUST use `--mount=type=secret`; ARG/ENV with credentials is rejected.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Skip when no Dockerfile is authored OR build is fully managed by an external buildpack.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.
